preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 35% to win, with Ecuador given a 34-percentage-point underdog case at 34%. The two outcomes sit in a narrow band, with draws assessed at 32%, making this a tightly competitive fixture by the statistical forecast.

The spread across outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty. Germany's modest four-point advantage over Ecuador masks a scenario in which a draw is nearly as likely as either team's outright victory. No outcome commands strong confidence; all three results fall within a plausible range, suggesting the teams are closely matched on the model's inputs.

The sportsbook consensus departs significantly from the model, assigning Germany a 56% win probability—roughly 21 percentage points higher than the statistical forecast—while cutting Ecuador to 19%. The market judges the match far less competitive. No Polymarket data is yet available. The dashboard does not claim to know which assessment is more accurate; users should weigh both sources against their own judgment.
